name: Total Daily Energy Expenditure Calculator
description: Industrial-grade TDEE calculator based on anthropometric inputs, activity factors, and metabolic adjustment formulas. Compliant with ISO 8996 (ergonomics of the thermal environment) and lean energy balance principles.

inputs.label: Age
inputs.businessContext: Age affects basal metabolic rate; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict equations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Age affects basal metabolic rate; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict equations.

inputs.label: Gender
inputs.businessContext: Gender-specific coefficients for BMR estimation per ISO 8996 and clinical standards.

businessContext_i18n.en: Gender-specific coefficients for BMR estimation per ISO 8996 and clinical standards.

inputs.label: Body Weight
inputs.businessContext: Primary driver of energy expenditure; used in all BMR formulas.

businessContext_i18n.en: Primary driver of energy expenditure; used in all BMR formulas.

inputs.label: Height
inputs.businessContext: Height influences lean body mass and BMR; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict.

businessContext_i18n.en: Height influences lean body mass and BMR; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict.

inputs.label: Activity Level
inputs.businessContext: Physical activity level (PAL) multiplier per WHO/FAO and ISO 8996 metabolic rate classification.

businessContext_i18n.en: Physical activity level (PAL) multiplier per WHO/FAO and ISO 8996 metabolic rate classification.

inputs.label: Body Fat Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Optional input for Katch-McArdle formula; improves accuracy for lean mass estimation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Optional input for Katch-McArdle formula; improves accuracy for lean mass estimation.

inputs.label: Athlete Mode
inputs.businessContext: Activates adjusted activity multipliers for high-frequency training (6+ sessions/week).

businessContext_i18n.en: Activates adjusted activity multipliers for high-frequency training (6+ sessions/week).

rules.message: Age must be between 10 and 120 years.
Weight must be between 20 and 300 kg.
Height must be between 50 and 250 cm.
Body fat percentage must be between 3% and 60%.
Athlete mode is incompatible with sedentary activity level.
Body fat percentage is recommended for Katch-McArdle formula accuracy.

thresholds.message: BMR is critically low; verify inputs.
TDEE exceeds typical range; consider professional assessment.
Very high energy expenditure may indicate overtraining risk.

formulas.name: BMR (Mifflin-St Jeor)
formulas.description: Most validated equation for general population per ISO 8996.
formulas.formula: if gender == 'male' then (10 * weight) + (6.25 * height) - (5 * age) + 5 else (10 * weight) + (6.25 * height) - (5 * age) - 161

formulas.name: BMR (Harris-Benedict)
formulas.description: Classic equation; used as secondary reference.
formulas.formula: if gender == 'male' then 88.362 + (13.397 * weight) + (4.799 * height) - (5.677 * age) else 447.593 + (9.247 * weight) + (3.098 * height) - (4.330 * age)

formulas.name: BMR (Katch-McArdle)
formulas.description: Uses lean body mass; requires body fat percentage.

formulas.name: Ensemble BMR
formulas.description: Weighted average of available BMR formulas for robustness.
formulas.formula: (bmr_mifflin * 0.5 + bmr_harris * 0.3 + (if bodyFatPercent > 0 then bmr_katch * 0.2 else 0)) / (0.5 + 0.3 + (if bodyFatPercent > 0 then 0.2 else 0))

formulas.name: Activity Factor
formulas.description: PAL multiplier based on activity level and athlete mode.
formulas.formula: case activityLevel when 'sedentary' then 1.2 when 'light' then 1.375 when 'moderate' then 1.55 when 'active' then 1.725 when 'veryActive' then 1.9 else 1.55 end + (if isAthlete then 0.1 else 0)

formulas.name: Total Daily Energy Expenditure
formulas.description: Primary result: BMR multiplied by activity factor.

formulas.name: TDEE (Confidence Adjusted)
formulas.description: TDEE with data quality adjustment based on input completeness.

primary.label: Total Daily Energy Expenditure
primary.description: Estimated total calories burned per day.

activityBurn.label: Activity Energy Expenditure
activityBurn.description: Additional calories burned through physical activity.

thermicEffect.label: Thermic Effect of Food
thermicEffect.description: Energy used for digestion (estimated as 10% of TDEE).

bmrMethod.label: Primary BMR Method
bmrMethod.description: Equation used for primary BMR calculation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Activity Underreporting
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Self-reported activity often overestimates; consider objective tracking.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Body Fat Estimation Error
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Visual or impedance methods have ±5% error; affects Katch-McArdle accuracy.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Age-Related Metabolic Decline
hiddenLossDrivers.description: BMR decreases ~1-2% per decade after age 20; not fully captured by equations.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Potential 50-150 kcal/day overestimation for older adults.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: TEF Variability
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Thermic effect varies by macronutrient composition; fixed 10% may not apply.

suggestedActions.label: Track Weekly Weight Trends
suggestedActions.description: Compare actual weight change to predicted TDEE; adjust intake by 100-200 kcal if needed.

suggestedActions.label: Use Objective PAL Measurement
suggestedActions.description: Wear a heart rate monitor or accelerometer for 7 days to refine activity factor.

suggestedActions.label: Reassess Body Fat via DEXA or Calipers
suggestedActions.description: Improve accuracy of Katch-McArdle BMR estimation.

suggestedActions.label: Adjust for Athlete Recovery Days
suggestedActions.description: On rest days, reduce activity multiplier by 0.2 to avoid overestimation.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjustment Factor
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Multiplier between 0.85 and 1.0 based on input completeness and consistency.
dataConfidenceAdjusted.calculation: 1.0 - (if bodyFatPercent == null then 0.05 else 0) - (if age < 18 || age > 80 then 0.03 else 0) - (if isAthlete && activityLevel == 'moderate' then 0.02 else 0)
